references.title: Variational Deep Knowledge Tracing for Language Learning

extracted.Student Performance Definition: Getting next word in the sequence correct
extracted.Students:
6000 language learners on SLAM dataset

Unmentioned number on HLR
extracted.Courses: 3 Languages on SLAM: English, Spanish and French
extracted.Flaw of Previous Models:
DKT approaches learn from raw data, meaning that they cannot capture latent student behaviors.

BKT is able to do that, but the model, on a results-based approach underperforms DKT.
extracted.Novelty of Model:
The model introduces a seq-to-seq form of knowledge modelling. While advantageous relative to DKT or BKT (theoretically), the sequence model has a drawback of being deterministic.

The authors also propose Variational Knowledge Tracing which adds a latent variable to each time step.
extracted.Preprocessing Details: In this experiment, each word in the sequence is understood as an exercise. Thus, the prediction task becomes predicting if the next word in the sentence will be correct or not.
extracted.Models:
SDKT,
VDKT,
Self-Ensemble of SDKT, Self-Ensemble of VDKT, GBoost Ensemble of SDKT, GBoost Ensemble of VDKT
extracted.Comments:
Work proposes a new approach that uses sequences to see if students understand gramatical rules. 

As a probabilistic model, VDKT encodes the understanding of these rules as a latent variable.
extracted.Limitations:
No Cross-Validation, 

Could be generalized for other contexts. 

Latent variables only capture information extractable from the learning trajectories - something other data sources could probably capture.

extracted.Student Performance Definition: Correctly answering the word being practiced
extracted.Features: [Sequence of previous words discovered on the platform]
extracted.Flaw of Previous Models:
DKT approaches learn from raw data, meaning that they cannot capture latent student behaviors.

BKT is able to do that, but the model, on a results-based approach underperforms DKT.
extracted.Novelty of Model: The model introduces a seq-to-seq form of knowledge modelling. While advantageous relative to DKT or BKT (theoretically), the sequence model has a drawback of being deterministic.
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Word discovery is understood as a learning section. Thus, the authors save the “sequence” of the previous exercises done.

Models use a Encoder-Decoder architecture.
extracted.Comments:
Work proposes a new approach that uses sequences to see if students understand gramatical rules. 

As a probabilistic model, VDKT encodes the understanding of these rules as a latent variable.
extracted.Limitations:
No Cross-Validation, 

Could be generalized for other contexts. 

Latent variables only capture information extractable from the learning trajectories - something other data sources could probably capture.

annotations.text: DKT is a state-of-the-art technique that predicts a learner’s performance based on observed learning history. However, learning history does not account for the whole picture: guessing, partial understanding, etc… To include a measurement of these elemente, the authors propose Variational Deep Knowledge Tracing (VDKT), which uses latent variables to incorporate their stochasticity.
OL a KT task, VDKT beats seq-to-seq DKT baselines and other KT models on different language learning datasets across different metrics.
